source:
Reports missing Javadoc comments and tags.

Example:


  /**
   * Missing "@param" is reported (if configured).
   */
  public void sample(int param){
  }

The quick-fixes add missing tag or missing Javadoc comment:


  /**
   * Missing "@param" is reported (if configured).
   * @param param
   */
  public void sample(int param){
  }

Inspection can be configured to ignore deprecated elements or simple accessor methods like getField() or setField().

You can also use options below to configure required tags and minimal required visibility for the specific code elements like method, field, class, package, module.
target:
Meldet fehlende Javadoc-Kommentare und Tags.

Beispiel:


  /**
   * Missing "@param" is reported (if configured).
   */
  public void sample(int param){
  }

Die Quick-Fixes fügen fehlende Tags oder fehlende Javadoc-Kommentare hinzu:


  /**
   * Missing "@param" is reported (if configured).
   * @param param
   */
  public void sample(int param){
  }

Die Inspection kann so konfiguriert werden, dass veraltete Elemente oder einfache Accessor-Methoden wie getField() oder setField() ignoriert werden.

Sie können auch die Optionen unten verwenden, um erforderliche Tags und die mindestens erforderliche Sichtbarkeit für bestimmte Code-Elemente wie Methode, Feld, Klasse, Package, Modul zu konfigurieren.

source:
Reports redundant Unit expressions.

Unit in Kotlin can be used as the return type of functions that do not return anything meaningful. The Unit type has only one possible value, which is the Unit object.

Examples:


  fun redundantA(): Unit {
      return Unit // redundant, 'Unit' is returned by default and matches the expected return type
  }

  fun requiredA(condition: Boolean): Any {
      if (condition) return "hello"
      return Unit // explicit 'Unit' is required since the expected type is 'Any'
  }

  fun redundantB(condition: Boolean): Any = if (condition) {
      fun ancillary(): Int = 1
      println("${ancillary()}")
      Unit // redundant since the last expression is already of type 'Unit'
  } else {
      println("else")
  }

  fun requiredB(condition: Boolean): Any = if (condition) {
      1024
      Unit // required, otherwise '1024' (Int) would be the return value
  } else {
      println("else")
  }
target:
Meldet redundante Unit-Ausdrücke.

Unit kann in Kotlin als Rückgabetyp von Funktionen verwendet werden, die nichts Sinnvolles zurückgeben. Der Unit-Typ hat nur einen möglichen Wert, nämlich das Unit-Objekt.

Beispiele:


  fun redundantA(): Unit {
      return Unit // redundant, 'Unit' is returned by default and matches the expected return type
  }

  fun requiredA(condition: Boolean): Any {
      if (condition) return "hello"
      return Unit // explicit 'Unit' is required since the expected type is 'Any'
  }

  fun redundantB(condition: Boolean): Any = if (condition) {
      fun ancillary(): Int = 1
      println("${ancillary()}")
      Unit // redundant since the last expression is already of type 'Unit'
  } else {
      println("else")
  }

  fun requiredB(condition: Boolean): Any = if (condition) {
      1024
      Unit // required, otherwise '1024' (Int) would be the return value
  } else {
      println("else")
  }

source:
Reports plain text links in Javadoc comments.

The quick-fix suggests to wrap the link in an <a> tag.

Example:


  class Main {
   /**
    * https://en.wikipedia.org/
    */
    void foo() {}
  }

After the quick-fix is applied:


  class Main {
   /**
    * <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/">https://en.wikipedia.org/</a>
    */
    void foo() {}
  }

New in 2022.1
target:
Meldet Klartext-Links in Javadoc-Kommentaren.

Der Quick-Fix schlägt vor, den Link in ein <a>-Tag einzuschließen.

Beispiel:


  class Main {
   /**
    * https://en.wikipedia.org/
    */
    void foo() {}
  }

Nach Anwenden des Quick-Fixes:


  class Main {
   /**
    * <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/">https://en.wikipedia.org/</a>
    */
    void foo() {}
  }

Neu in 2022.1
Erzählen Sie uns von Ihrer Erfahrung
